title: video-to-gif JSON manifest
description: Schema for JSON batch manifests (spec section 10). Manifest values are treated purely as data: no shell expressions, no environment expansion, no command substitution, no code (spec SEC-009). Unknown fields/columns are warned about rather than rejected, so additionalProperties is left permissive.

schemaVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Must be 1 for this release.

input.description: Local path to the source video. Remote URLs are not supported in v0.1.0.

outputDirectory.description: Directory GIFs are written to; created if missing. Defaults to ./output.

continueOnError.description: When true, a runtime failure on one clip does not stop remaining clips.

collisionPolicy.description: How to handle an existing output file. Engine policies: fail (default), overwrite, unique, skip.

allowUpscale.description: When true, the engine may upscale a source narrower than the profile maximum width. Off by default.

clips.description: One or more clip definitions. Clip-level fields override top-level fields.

timestamp.description: A timestamp: a non-negative number of seconds (optional fraction) or a string in seconds, MM:SS, or HH:MM:SS form with optional .mmm.

duration.description: A strictly positive duration: a number of seconds (optional fraction) or a timestamp-format string. Normalized internally to integer milliseconds.

loop.description: Loop behavior. 'forever' loops infinitely; 'once' equals a count of 1; an integer N >= 1 plays N times. 0 is rejected.

width.description: Maximum output width in pixels (aspect ratio preserved; no upscaling by default).

fps.description: Target frames per second (capped at the source frame rate).

colors.description: Maximum palette colors (GIF supports up to 256).

clip.description: A single clip: requires start plus at least one of end or duration. When both end and duration are present the engine additionally validates that they resolve to the same end timestamp (start + duration == end); JSON Schema cannot compute that, so it is enforced by the engine and mismatched pairs are rejected.

name.description: Optional output name (without extension). MUST NOT contain path separators or escape the output directory.
